title: leonurus quinquelobatus
intro: Leonurus quinquelobatus, also known as Sweet Woodruff or Bugle, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it a popular remedy for digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Leonurus quinquelobatus include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from headaches and fever. Herbal preparations of Leonurus quinquelobatus are typically made by infusing the plant in hot water to create a tea or by using it in tincture form, with recommended doses varying depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
